commit.message:
fix: use non-empty password for throwaway key — Tauri CLI can't decrypt empty-pw keys

Tauri CLI bug: cannot decrypt scrypt keys with empty passwords regardless of key source.
Use a random non-empty password for the throwaway key and match it with PASSWORD env var.
rsign2 step still replaces the .sig with the real signature.

Fix auto-spawn deadlock and improve game quality

commit.message:
Fix: Compare FINGERPRINT_HASH instead of file change for native build detection (#9)

The previous detection logic triggered native builds whenever the
.fingerprint file changed, including RUNTIME_VERSION-only bumps that
don't require a rebuild. Now compares the actual FINGERPRINT_HASH values
between commits. Also removes placeholder ascAppId values from eas.json
submit profiles — EAS auto-detects via bundleIdentifier.

commit.message:
fix(ci): add [workspace] to generated Cargo.toml to prevent workspace resolution errors

Generated crates in generated/ were inheriting the parent workspace,
causing "current package believes it's in a workspace when it's not"
errors during CI. Fix by:
- Adding [workspace] to each generated program Cargo.toml
- Adding [workspace] to each generated registry Cargo.toml
- Adding exclude = ["generated"] to root Cargo.toml
- Fixing formatting in test files from previous commit

commit.message:
Fix Edge Function error visibility by always returning HTTP 200 (#6)

The Supabase JS SDK consumes the Response body of non-2xx responses
before client code can read them, making error messages invisible.
Changed Edge Function to always return 200 with errors in JSON body,
and simplified client-side error handling accordingly.

fix: force-replace pnpm symlinks with real files for prisma resolution

pnpm install creates symlinks at packages/database/node_modules/@prisma/client
pointing to the .pnpm store. Prisma's custom resolver cannot follow these symlinks.
Previous attempt skipped the copy because `-d` returns true for symlink-to-directory.

Fix: unconditionally rm -rf the symlink and cp -rL real files.
Also switched from npx to pnpm exec for prisma generate.

commit.message:
fix: move eslint-disable-next-line to target async arrow function

Prettier reformatted the file and split the export across lines,
causing the disable comment to target the wrong line. Move it to
directly above the async arrow function where the warnings fire.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(web): show error details for failed tool calls (#490)

* feat(web): show error details and input params for failed tool calls

Failed tool calls in the gateway UI previously showed only a red X icon
with an empty expandable body. This change:

- Adds optional `error` and `parameters` fields to `ToolCompleted` SSE
  events so the browser receives failure details in real-time
- Auto-expands failed tool cards to make errors immediately visible
- Adds `StatusUpdate::tool_completed()` constructor that centralizes
  the 5 duplicated construction sites and applies `redact_params()` to
  prevent sensitive values (e.g. secret_save's "value" param) from
  leaking through SSE broadcasts
- Adds `sensitive_params()` trait method to `Tool` for declaring which
  parameters must be redacted before logging, hooks, and UI display
- Adds `redact_params()` utility and wires it through hooks, approvals,
  ActionRecord storage, and debug logs in dispatcher/worker
- Adds `SecretListTool` and `SecretDeleteTool` for LLM-driven secret
  management (values never returned, only names/metadata)
- Fixes auth flow: setup-only extensions show configure modal instead
  of OAuth card; auth_completed SSE dismisses both UI paths
- CI: release workflow creates PR instead of pushing directly to main
- Registry: MissingChecksum error enables source fallback for
  bootstrapping when checksums haven't been populated yet

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: apply cargo fmt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: keep original params in PendingApproval for execution, redact only for display

Address two PR review comments:

1. execute_chat_tool_standalone now redacts sensitive params before logging,
   matching the pattern already used in worker.rs.

2. PendingApproval previously stored redacted parameters, which meant
   approved tool calls received "[REDACTED]" instead of the actual values.
   Add a display_parameters field for UI/logs and keep parameters as the
   original values used for execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- worker.rs: redact sensitive params before BeforeToolCall hook, matching
  dispatcher.rs — hooks in the autonomous job path now receive redacted
  params instead of raw values
- registry.rs: fix docstring for register_secrets_tools (list, delete,
  not save/list/delete — no SecretSaveTool is registered)
- app.js: fix double toast/loadExtensions in submitConfigureModal —
  for non-OAuth success the auth_completed SSE already handles both,
  so skip them in the HTTP response handler to avoid duplicates

[skip-regression-check]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(chat): upgrade to Tool Use Agent with 4-provider LLM fallback

Replace svc-llm-router passthrough with direct Anthropic API calls
supporting tool_use for real data queries. Add 7 tools (document stats,
pipeline KPI, policy/skill/term search, analysis summary) via service
bindings to 6 internal services.

4-provider fallback chain: Anthropic → OpenAI → Google → Workers AI.
Anthropic/OpenAI/Google support function calling (tools preserved),
Workers AI is text-only free-tier last resort. Fallback triggers on
402/429/500/503/529 errors.

Frontend shows tool usage badges on assistant messages.

commit.message:
Sprints 9-11: security hardening, code review fixes, JWT consolidation

Sprint 9 — Security Hardening:
- Auth hardening: JWT secret validation in production, demo user guard, verify_auth always requires credentials
- User routes: reset token no longer leaked in HTTP response, field injection blocked via allowlist, password complexity validation
- IDOR fix: data rights ownership enforcement with admin override
- Input validation: webhook SSRF protection, request-ID injection prevention, metrics auth
- Audit PII pepper separated from JWT secret, API key expiration checking

Sprint 10 — Code Review Fixes:
- Shared bypass_auth fixture in conftest.py replacing 7 inline copies
- Centralized is_admin() helper and set_user_role() privileged function
- Bounded reset tokens (OrderedDict, 10k cap), precompiled regex, config constants extracted

Sprint 11 — Code Review Fixes:
- SSRF: replaced is_private/is_loopback/is_link_local with not addr.is_global (catches carrier-grade NAT, multicast, reserved)
- JWT consolidation: require_role and refresh_token now delegate to verify_auth (single source of truth)
- _api_keys bounded at 10k with lazy deletion of expired entries
- Dashboard re-reads user after mutations to prevent stale data
- Test string literals replaced with _DEFAULT_JWT_SECRET/_DEFAULT_PII_PEPPER constants

735 tests passing, 0 architecture violations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  to the right of the logo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Teleport Staff recharge cost via skills.txt cost mult/add

Set cost mult=3000 and cost add=0 on the Teleport skill row in skills.txt
when the Teleport Staff starting item is enabled. The recharge cost at
vendors is driven by the skill's own cost columns, not uniqueitems.txt,
so the item's 1-gold floor was caused by Teleport's blank cost columns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix random opponent sim levels: use 5-25 sims instead of scaling to NN level

The random opponent was using 32-128 sims (25-100% of NN sims), making it
equally strong in search depth. At random init, NN played ~60% vs random,
which was entirely from Gumbel's structural advantage, not from learning.

Fixed to use 5-25 sims so the NN has a clear search advantage, producing
more decisive games and stronger learning signal for bootstrapping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add scroll-responsive navbar with blur and accent glow

Navbar detects scroll past 50px and transitions to stronger backdrop blur,
accent-tinted border, subtle shadow, and tighter vertical padding. All
properties animate over 300ms for a smooth scroll response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: default I2V templates to fp8 base model precision

I2V's 36-channel input (vs T2V's 16) makes OOM likely on 80GB GPUs
with bf16 base weights. fp8 quantization saves ~14GB with minimal
quality impact, making it the better default for I2V training.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
KEPOS SCROLL FIX — track vv.offsetTop + reset document scroll

Two complementary fixes for the white-space / scroll-offscreen bug:

1. rootY state (vv.offsetTop): When iOS keyboard opens it scrolls the
   document body to try to reveal the focused input. This shifts the
   visual viewport down, leaving our fixed root (top:0) above the
   visible area. By driving `top: rootY` from vv.offsetTop we follow
   the visual viewport, so the root always starts at the top of what
   the user can see.

2. window.scroll resetScroll listener: Calls window.scrollTo(0,0)
   immediately whenever the document scrolls. This prevents iOS from
   accumulating a scroll offset that would expose body background below
   the root container (the "white block" below the textarea).

Together: root fills exactly vv.height × vv.offsetTop (the visible
area above the keyboard). No white space, no scrolling off-screen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
